model_input:
You tried to click at (300, 300) but that position was outside the target element. The target is still: Click the Beta project card

Look at the screenshot again and call click_at(x, y, confidence) with corrected coordinates.

Call click_at().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click on the "Beta" project card.
Looking at the screenshot, there are several project cards: Alpha, Beta, Gamma, Delta, Epsilon, Zeta.
The Beta card is the second one in the first row.
It has a green border.
Its center seems to be around x=380, y=275.

Let's refine the coordinates for the Beta card.
The card starts around x=268 and ends around x=498.
The card starts around y=185 and ends around y=365.
Center x = (268 + 498) / 2 = 383
Center y = (185 + 365) / 2 = 275

Let's check the coordinates of the other cards to be sure.
Alpha: [34, 185, 258, 365] -> center (146, 275)
Beta: [268, 185, 498, 365] -> center (383, 275)
Gamma: [508, 185, 732, 365] -> center (620, 275)
Delta: [34, 395, 258, 575] -> center (146, 485)
Epsilon: [268, 395, 498, 575] -> center (383, 485)
Zeta: [508, 395, 732, 575] -> center (620, 485)

The target is the "Beta project card".
I will click at (383, 275).
